title: Reflected XSS
description: A search page that displays the search term back to the user.

stages.name: Find the vulnerability
stages.instruction: Identify the vulnerability and explain why it's dangerous.
stages.target: The 'q' parameter is directly embedded into the HTML response without sanitization, leading to Reflected XSS.

stages.name: Exploit it
stages.instruction: Provide a simple payload to trigger an alert box in the browser.

stages.name: Patch it
stages.instruction: How would you fix this code to prevent the injection?
stages.target: Use a templating engine like Jinja2 which escapes HTML by default, or manually escape the input using a library like 'markupsafe'.

stages.name: Explain it
stages.instruction: What is the difference between Reflected and Stored XSS?
stages.target: Reflected XSS is when the payload is delivered via a URL and 'reflected' off the server. Stored XSS is when the payload is permanently stored on the server (e.g., in a database).

hints: The search query is put directly into the HTML.
HTML tags can be used as part of the input.
